acceptance.assertion: Opportunistic, required, strict, live-first, and external-safe rLens modes are represented deterministically.
A required/strict task without rLens context records a machine-readable skip or block reason.

goal: Add Bureau task-class policy so repo/PR/roadmap/architecture work records rLens refs or explicit skip reasons while live-first tasks remain unblocked.

metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #219: task schemas now support deterministic rlens_policy modes, Bureau evaluates policy into rlens_context_policy, required/strict/external-safe tasks without context ref or skip reason are blocked, and envelopes/receipts/handoffs record the machine-readable policy result.
validation: Bureau PR #219 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local: PYTHONPATH=src make validate -> ruff pass, 340 tests passed, bureau check valid.

verified_acceptance.mode-policy: opportunistic, required, strict, live-first and external-safe are schema-bounded and evaluated deterministically into rlens_context_policy.
verified_acceptance.skip-reason: required/strict/external-safe tasks without rlens_context_ref and without rlens_policy.skip_reason produce a machine-readable block reason; tasks with skip_reason record skipped policy in envelope/receipt.

metadata.coordination_note: PR #217 was merged before this closeout because it touched registry/queue.json and was already green/mergeable; closeout is based on the updated Bureau main.

title: Enforce rLens context refs in Bureau modes
